message: defaultProcessRunner starts the adapter subprocess with cmd.Start() and kills its process group on Close/handshake-failure, but cmd.Wait() is never called anywhere. A SIGKILL'd child that is never waited on becomes a defunct/zombie process that persists until the parent process exits. For the short-lived debate CLI this is reaped at process exit, but looper is intended as a reusable discussion-loop library/runtime; a long-running host opens and closes many sessions (and even failed Opens kill in openAt) and would accumulate zombies, eventually risking process-table exhaustion. This production path is exercised only by the gated (skipped) integration test, so the default suite does not catch it.
trigger: Every Open of a real (non-test) ACP session: after the subprocess is killed it is never reaped, leaving a defunct process for the lifetime of the host process; accumulates across many sessions in a long-running host.
fix_direction: Call cmd.Wait() after kill (e.g. in a goroutine, or synchronously inside the kill func after sending SIGKILL) so the child is reaped; ensure Wait runs on both the Close path and the openAt handshake-failure path.

message: The ACP transport silently drops spec.System. The runner populates Spec.System from each persona's system prompt (internal/debate/runner/runner.go:87 and :146), and the prompt builder (internal/debate/prompt/prompt.go) does not embed the persona system prompt into the Send content — it renders only moderator rules, the shared brief, the board, and the signal instruction. The ACP transport never reads spec.System in buildCmd, NewSession, or Send, so when a real debate runs against claude-agent-acp/codex-acp every persona receives identical context with no persona identity/role, collapsing the distinct-persona behavior the debate depends on.
trigger: Any real debate run via the acp backend where personas define distinct system prompts: the per-persona system prompt never reaches the model.
fix_direction: Deliver spec.System to the adapter — e.g. prepend it to the first Send prompt for the session (and re-send it as the lead of replay during recovery), or document explicitly that System delivery is out of scope for this slice and why.
uncertainty: The contract acceptance criteria never mention System delivery and ACP NewSession exposes no system-prompt field, so this may be an intentionally-deferred limitation rather than a bug; a human should decide whether it is in scope for this slice.

message: Sealed-mode temporary directory is leaked. resolveCwd(sealed=true) creates a fresh temp dir via os.MkdirTemp("", "debate-sealed-*") and stores it in acpSession.cwd, but Close() only kills the subprocess and nothing ever calls os.RemoveAll on that directory. Each sealed session therefore leaves an empty debate-sealed-* directory in the temp dir that persists after the process exits, accumulating across runs.
trigger: Every Open with spec.ReadOnly=true (--sealed): a temp directory is created and never deleted.
fix_direction: Track whether the cwd was a transport-created temp dir and os.RemoveAll it in Close() (once, after kill); guard against removing the process cwd in the grounded case.

message: classifyConnErr maps every JSON-RPC -32603 (Internal error) to ErrTransportDrop (retryable). Because agent-side handler errors are commonly surfaced as -32603, a terminal/permanent agent failure reported as a generic handler error is classified retryable and triggers an unnecessary recovery: close, reopen, and full prior-history replay, before the same condition re-fails and the error finally surfaces. The contract's intended mapping puts protocol errors in the terminal bucket; this widens the retryable bucket to all internal errors.
trigger: A non-transient agent-side error that the adapter reports as a generic JSON-RPC -32603 internal error (rather than via a non-end_turn stop reason or a distinct error code): it is retried once with a full-history replay before surfacing.
fix_direction: If the SDK distinguishes connection-drop/peer-disconnect from generic internal errors (e.g. via error data, wrapped io errors, or a distinct code), narrow the retryable -32603 mapping to genuine drops and route other internal errors to ErrClientError (terminal).
uncertainty: Could not read the acp-go-sdk source (out-of-repo reads denied), so the exact set of conditions the adapters surface as -32603 vs distinct codes is unverified; the practical harm is one wasted reopen+replay rather than incorrect output.

message: The ACP transport silently drops spec.System (the persona's system-prompt body). buildCmd consumes spec.Model and spec.Effort, openAt's NewSessionRequest sets only Cwd/McpServers, and sendOnce's PromptRequest sends only the bare per-turn prompt; spec.System is never referenced. The runner sets System=p.System for every participant and the synthesizer, and the orchestrate PromptBuilder does not include persona identity, so a real claude-agent-acp/codex-acp debate gives every participant identical moderator+brief prompts with no persona instructions, undermining the contract goal of differentiated multi-persona debate.
trigger: Running a real debate via claude-agent-acp or codex-acp with personas whose .md body defines distinct roles/stances.
fix_direction: Convey spec.System to the agent: prepend it as a leading content block on the first Prompt turn (or via the adapter's system/instructions mechanism if the SDK/adapter supports one) so each persona's instructions reach the model.
uncertainty: Whether conveying spec.System is in-scope for this slice or a deliberate later-slice deferral; the explicit acceptance criteria do not mention the system prompt, though the goal ('real debates') and the runner already wiring System into the Spec imply it.

message: No deterministic test asserts the process-group setting. Acceptance criterion #9 states the injectable runner exists 'so deterministic tests assert the process-group setting and that Close performs cleanup', but Setpgid:true is set only inside defaultProcessRunner, which the fake-runner tests never invoke, and the ProcessRunner signature (dir,name,args,env) does not surface SysProcAttr. Close cleanup is asserted via killCount, but the process-group setting is unasserted and unassertable through the current runner abstraction.
fix_direction: Add a test exercising defaultProcessRunner that asserts SysProcAttr.Setpgid (e.g. spawn a trivial process and check its pgid), or extend the ProcessRunner abstraction to surface the process-group intent so the fake runner can record and assert it.

message: Open's spawn and ACP-handshake failure paths are untested. openAt handles three failure branches — spawn error -> ErrClientError, Initialize error -> kill()+classified error, NewSession error -> kill()+classified error — but the fake agent always succeeds at Initialize and NewSession and the fake runner never returns an error. The acceptance criterion 'a spawn or handshake failure returns a classified error and leaves no orphaned subprocess' (the kill() cleanup on failure) has no deterministic coverage and could regress silently.
trigger: A spawn failure or an ACP handshake failure (Initialize or NewSession returning an error) during Open.
fix_direction: Add deterministic tests: (a) a runner that returns an error and assert Open returns a classified ErrClientError; (b) a fake agent whose Initialize or NewSession returns an error and assert Open returns a classified error AND that kill was invoked (no orphaned subprocess).

message: Recovery tests do not verify that prior prompts are replayed in order or with the correct content. fakeAgent.Prompt discards the incoming p.Prompt and responds purely by positional scenario index, so TestSend_RecoveryWithHistoryReplay asserts only the final response (r3). A recovery that replayed wrong prompt text, replayed empty strings, or reordered the history would still pass. The contract explicitly requires replaying prior prompts in order.
trigger: Recovery replay sends prior prompts out of order or with altered content.
fix_direction: Record each PromptRequest's text in fakeAgent and assert in the recovery test that the replayed prompts equal the prior history in order, followed by the re-sent current prompt.

message: clientImpl callback methods have no deterministic test. The fake agent only calls asc.SessionUpdate and never invokes the client side, so RequestPermission's branching (no options -> cancelled vs. select-first-option) and the read-only WriteTextFile denial are never exercised in the default suite.
trigger: An adapter requests permission (with zero or multiple options) or attempts a file write/read during a session.
fix_direction: Add unit tests calling clientImpl.RequestPermission with zero options (expect cancelled) and with multiple options (expect first selected), and WriteTextFile (expect denial error).

message: classifyConnErr and stopReasonErr branch coverage is thin. Only the InternalError(-32603)->ErrTransportDrop path is exercised. The auth(-32000)->ErrAuth, cancelled(-32800)->ErrCanceled, io.EOF/broken-pipe->ErrTransportDrop, and default->ErrClientError branches, plus stopReasonErr's Cancelled case, are untested, even though the contract lists auth->terminal as a required classification.
trigger: ACP returns an auth/cancelled/protocol RequestError or an IO-level (EOF/broken-pipe) error, or a turn stops with reason cancelled.
fix_direction: Add table-driven tests for classifyConnErr using synthetic *acpsdk.RequestError values per code and IO errors, asserting transport.Classify's Retryable/Kind; add a StopReasonCancelled case for stopReasonErr.

message: outcomeString is a no-op pass-through: every switch branch, including the default, returns the input `reason` unchanged. The function adds no normalization or transformation and could be replaced by using result.Outcome.Reason directly at the call site.
fix_direction: Delete outcomeString and inline result.Outcome.Reason at the call site (main.go:170), or if normalization is genuinely intended later, implement it now rather than leaving an empty extension point.

message: The contract acceptance criterion requires documenting claude-agent-acp write-prevention as best-effort governed by the adapter permission layer, but the code documents it as an absolute guarantee. The WriteTextFile comment states 'the agent must not write files' and its error says 'transport is read-only', while RequestPermission allows the first permission option for any request and the claude branch of buildCmd adds no read-only flag — so the agent can still write via its own tools. The required best-effort caveat is absent and the existing comment is misleading.
trigger: always — the documentation is present in source as written and does not match the contract requirement
fix_direction: Reword the WriteTextFile/RequestPermission comments (and optionally the claude branch of buildCmd or the package doc) to state that claude write-prevention is best-effort: the ACP WriteTextFile client call is denied, but the agent may still write via its own tools because RequestPermission allows options and claude exposes no read-only adapter flag; prevention is governed by the adapter permission layer, as the contract requires.

message: TestSend_MultipleTurns is the test for the 'persistent session' acceptance criterion (subprocess/session created once in Open, reused across Sends), but it only asserts the returned content (turn1/turn2) and never asserts a single spawn/session. Because newFakeRunner reuses the same fakeAgent across spawns and scenarios advance by a positional callIdx, a regression that respawns the adapter (and re-runs Initialize/NewSession) on each Send would not change the asserted content and the test would still pass. The persistence property therefore has no deterministic guard in the default suite.
trigger: A regression that recreates the subprocess and ACP session on each Send (breaking the persistent-session criterion) is not detected by the default suite.
fix_direction: After the two Sends in TestSend_MultipleTurns, assert len(state.spawns)==1 (and/or len(agent.newSessCwds)==1) so the test fails if the session is recreated per Send.

message: TestClose_Idempotent only asserts 'killCount > 1' is an error, which permits killCount==0. It therefore verifies idempotency (no double-kill) but never verifies that Close actually terminates the subprocess. The only test asserting killCount>=1 (TestSend_RetryableDropRecovery) reaches kill via s.closeInternal() during recovery, not via Close(). As a result, a regression where Close() stops invoking kill (leaking the subprocess) would pass the entire default suite, leaving the 'Close terminates the subprocess and its process group' half of acceptance criterion #8 without a positive assertion.
trigger: A regression where Close() no longer invokes kill (subprocess not terminated on Close) is not caught.
fix_direction: Add a positive assertion that a single Close() produces killCount==1 (subprocess terminated), in addition to the existing idempotency upper-bound check.

message: docs/DESIGN.md §7 "Окружение" is the only place the docs enumerate per-transport environment, and it is now stale for the acp backend that this slice makes reachable. It states the acp adapter "версия запинена" (version pinned), but the implementation defaults both adapters to a floating @latest tag (internal/backend/acp/acp.go:32-33), and it omits the two new user-facing override env vars DEBATE_CLAUDE_AGENT_ACP_PACKAGE / DEBATE_CODEX_ACP_PACKAGE (acp.go:27-28,126-139). With no README or --help listing of env vars, this design-doc line is the only environment reference, and it now misinforms a reader on both the version-pinning behavior and the available override knobs.
trigger: A user or operator consults docs/DESIGN.md §7 'Okruzhenie' to learn how the acp adapters are versioned or whether the npm package can be overridden after this slice makes claude-agent-acp/codex-acp reachable.
fix_direction: Update docs/DESIGN.md §7 (and SLICES.md §5 'пин версии адаптера') to reflect the shipped behavior: adapters default to @latest (not pinned) and the package is overridable via DEBATE_CLAUDE_AGENT_ACP_PACKAGE / DEBATE_CODEX_ACP_PACKAGE — OR, if pinning is the intended contract, this is a code/scope matter and the doc fix is to confirm pinning once the code pins; documentation should match whichever the scope lens settles on.
uncertainty: docs/DESIGN.md and docs/SLICES.md are forward-looking design drafts created in a single upfront commit (e3bc435) and not updated by any prior slice, so maintainers may intentionally not track adapter knobs in them; and the pinned-vs-@latest divergence may be resolved by pinning in code (scope lens) rather than amending the doc, which would change what the correct documentation says.
